content: When you put money in a bank, it can grow all by itself over time. This happens because of something called interest, which is like a thank-you gift from the bank for letting them use your money. For example, if you save 10 dollars and the bank gives you 1 dollar as interest, you will have 11 dollars after some time. That’s where compound interest gets exciting because the bank gives you interest not only on your original 10 dollars but also on the 1 dollar you earned! Over time, this means your savings can grow faster and faster, like a snowball rolling down a hill getting bigger and bigger. The longer you keep your money in the bank, the more it grows because each time you get interest, it adds to the total amount. Even if you start with a small amount, compound interest can help your money grow a lot if you’re patient. Usually, you can take your money out whenever you want, but the longer you leave it, the more interest you earn. So, saving money with compound interest is like planting a tiny seed that grows into a big tree over time.
